prompt: I just created a new Python repo for my SaaS side project (Django + Postgres, Python 3.12). It's just me right now but I'm hoping to bring on 1-2 contractors in a few months. Customers will pay for access so I can't have broken deployments. Currently there's nothing set up — just a .gitignore and a requirements.txt. What should I set up to protect my repo?

assertions.text: Output recommends a named tier (Minimal, Standard, or Strict) — not just a list of tools
Tier is Standard (not Minimal, not Strict) — solo with paying customers and contractors incoming warrants Standard
Output includes a .pre-commit-config.yaml snippet with at least black and ruff
Output includes a GitHub Actions CI workflow snippet
Output includes a PR template or explains how to create one
Output includes effort estimates (e.g. '~5 min') for at least half the items
Output explains WHY each major item matters (not just what it is)
Output ends with or includes order-of-operations guidance (pre-commit → branch protection → CI → security)
